title: Chapel Programming Language Cheat Sheet
description: Quick reference for Chapel programming language syntax and commands.

commands.description: Declare a variable with an integer value
commands.scenario: Declare a variable with an integer value

commands.command: proc add(a: int, b: int): int { return a + b; }
commands.description: Define a simple function that returns an integer
commands.scenario: Define a simple function that returns an integer

commands.description: Iterate over a range of numbers
commands.scenario: Iterate over a range of numbers

commands.command: if x > 0 then writeln("Positive"); else writeln("Non-positive");
commands.description: Conditional statement with if-else

commands.command: var i = 0; while i < 5 do { writeln(i); i += 1; }
commands.description: While loop example

commands.command: select x { when 1 do writeln("One"); otherwise writeln("Other"); }
commands.description: Select case using select statement
Declare and initialize an array
Use a tuple to store multiple values
commands.scenario: Use a tuple to store multiple values

commands.command: record Point { var x: real; var y: real; } var p = new Point(1.0, 2.0);
commands.description: Define a record type and create an instance
commands.scenario: Define a record type and create an instance

commands.description: Parallel loop using forall

commands.command: coforall loc in Locales do on loc do writeln("Hello from ", loc.name);
commands.description: Coforall loop for parallel execution
Sync variable for synchronization

commands.command: var f = open("file.txt", iomode.r); for line in f { writeln(line); } f.close();
commands.description: Read from a file line by line
commands.scenario: Read from a file line by line

commands.command: var f = open("output.txt", iomode.w); f.writeln("Hello, World!"); f.close();
commands.description: Write to a file

commands.command: var f = open("log.txt", iomode.a); f.writeln("New log entry"); f.close();
commands.description: Append to a file
Displays the current version of the Chapel compiler and build information.
Compiles the program with debug symbols enabled, useful for GDB/LLDB debugging.
Enables aggressive optimization flags to improve runtime performance for HPC workloads.
Specifies the target architecture and operating system for cross-compilation.
Creates a distributed associative array (dmap) for sparse data structures across locales.
